claim: Independent cross-check confirms: YOLO demand router evidence agent blocking logic is correctly implemented in router.ts with comprehensive test coverage in demand-router.test.ts, and status note filtering is properly implemented in both router.ts and evidence-dispatch.ts.

evidence.summary: evidenceResultBlockingIssues independently verified: creates blockers from error codes (489-495), missing items (496-502), and clarify/block/failed recommendations (503-509)
evidence.why: Direct read confirms the core blocking logic matches the explorer's claim exactly.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness calls evidenceResultBlockingIssues(result) for each evidence result
evidence.why: Confirms the blocking function is actually invoked in the PRD readiness check.

evidence.summary: prd_ready is set to blockers.length === 0, meaning PRD readiness is only true when zero blockers exist
evidence.why: This is the critical enforcement mechanism that prevents PRD generation with unresolved blockers.

evidence.summary: isNonMissingStatusItem pattern matches 'no missing data identified' (line 102) and 'all required evidence tasks completed' (line 103) patterns
evidence.why: Confirms the regex patterns correctly identify status notes versus actual missing data.

evidence.summary: sanitizeMissing filters out isNonMissingStatusItem items from missing arrays
evidence.why: Cross-check confirms status note filtering is implemented in the dispatch layer as well.

evidence.summary: Test asserts prd_ready is false when missing data and clarify recommendations exist, and checks for EVIDENCE_AGENT_MISSING and EVIDENCE_AGENT_CLARIFICATION_REQUIRED blocker codes
evidence.why: Test evidence independently validates the blocking behavior works as specified.

risks: If evidence agents return malformed results with unexpected field structures, the blocking logic might not catch all cases
Edge cases in status note pattern matching could evolve to allow false negatives if new status note formats are introduced
The implementation relies on correct agent role names; if role naming conventions change, the blocking checks could fail

result.notes: Independent cross-check of all explorer claims confirms complete alignment. The evidence agent blocking logic in router.ts correctly enforces that missing data, clarify requests, and block recommendations prevent PRD readiness. Status note filtering is properly implemented in both router.ts and evidence-dispatch.ts. Test coverage validates the expected blocking behavior. No conflicts found with the explorer's findings.
